steps.title: Separate estimate from certainty
steps.content: A random sample can estimate a population percentage, but it does not prove the exact percentage for all adults in the city.

steps.title: Check the three claims
steps.content: Statement I is not guaranteed because the sample percentage need not equal the population percentage. Statement II is not guaranteed because a second random sample can vary. Statement III is not guaranteed because a different city is a different population.

steps.title: Choose the only guaranteed option
steps.content:
Since none of the three statements must be true, the choice that says none is the only valid conclusion.
The answer is A.
